task.title: Deploy and version a rule set
task.shortdesc: Deploying a rule set means committing it to your documentation repository, verifying it does not create unexpected failures across existing content, and communicating any new requirements to your authors.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Deploy a new or updated markdown-validator rule set by committing it to version control, running a compliance report, and communicating changes to your authors.

prereq.p: All new rules are tested. See Test a rule in the REPL and Test a workflow in the REPL. Review governance principles before deploying: Rule set governance and versioning.

step.cmd: Run the rule set across the entire documentation repository and save the results.

info.codeblock: md-validate validate docs/ --rules rules/tutorial.json \ --format csv --output reports/pre-deploy-scan/
info.p: Review the CSV reports to understand the blast radius of the new rules. How many articles fail? Which authors are affected?

step.cmd: If the number of failures is large, change new Required rules to Suggested before deploying.

info.p: Deploying a Required rule that fails for 200 articles creates immediate pipeline failures for all authors. Start as Suggested, give authors time to comply, then promote to Required. See Rule set governance and versioning.

info.codeblock:
## 2026-03-20
### Added - Rule 12: `description-under-160-chars` (Suggested) — description field must be under 160 characters for SEO.
### Changed - Rule 3: `ms-date-must-be-fresh` promoted from Suggested to Required. Authors had 60 days to update stale dates.

step.cmd: Notify your author team of the changes before committing.

ul.li: Which rules are new (with their mitigation messages)
Which Suggested rules are being promoted to Required, and when
The date the change takes effect
How to run the validation locally to check their articles

step.cmd: Commit the updated rule set and changelog.

info.codeblock: git add rules/tutorial.json rules/CHANGELOG.md git commit -m "rules: add description length check (Suggested)" git push

step.stepresult: The CI pipeline uses the new rule set on all subsequent pull requests.
